era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt (Dynasty VI) is in its final phase. Naram-Sin's Akkad Empire dominates Mesopotamia and the Levant. The Harappan civilization is at its mature peak. This period represents a moment of relative stability in the Old World, though underlying stresses were accumulating.

events.title: Pepi II's reign: late Old Kingdom Egypt
events.description: Pepi II continued to rule Egypt during this year. His reign, whether it lasted 10 or 64 years, was characterized by the gradual erosion of central authority and the rise of provincial power. The pharaonic state remained functional, but the mechanisms of centralization that had characterized the earlier Old Kingdom were weakening.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for this year. The dating is conventional and carries ±50–100 year uncertainty.

events.title: Naram-Sin's Akkad Empire: imperial consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continued during this year. The Akkad Empire was at its territorial and administrative peak. Akkadian language and administrative practices were being imposed across the empire. The empire's stability, however, was fragile; internal resistance and external pressure from the Gutians would eventually lead to its collapse.

sources.name: Akkadian royal inscriptions and administrative texts

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but no specific events are recorded for this particular year.

events.title: Harappan civilization: mature phase continuation
events.description: The Harappan civilization continued to flourish during this year. Urban centers were fully developed, trade networks were active, and the civilization's characteristic material culture (seals, pottery, weights) was in use. The civilization's script, though undeciphered, was in use for administrative and commercial purposes.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The Harappan civilization's existence and chronology are well-established archaeologically.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are recorded for this year in any tradition.
historiographic_note: This year is undocumented in the historical record. The entries represent inferences from broader patterns in the archaeological and epigraphic record.
